recording_notes: Phone filming monitor — closer framing than V11/V12, better image quality. PURPLE/PINK suture used (different from black suture in V1-V10). Suture debris visible on tape from prior attempts. Best monitor-recorded image quality in the series so far.

frame_analyses.description: At 7 seconds, both instruments in field near drain. Pink/purple curved suture clearly visible draped across drain and block. Needle loading in progress — fast entry.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Fast entry at ~5 seconds. Purple suture is much more visible than the black suture used in V1-V10, improving assessment accuracy.

frame_analyses.description: At 28 seconds, both instruments at the drain. Suture has been placed through both sides of the drain — purple suture threads visible exiting both sides. Completing placement.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement completed in approximately 25 seconds — matching V12's record. Fast, confident needle driving.

frame_analyses.description: At 42 seconds, first throw in progress. Both suture ends (pink/purple) clearly visible extending from the drain. Instruments wrapping suture for surgeon's knot. The drain slit is still visible and open.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw wrapping. The purple suture makes the throw mechanics much more visible than in previous videos.

frame_analyses.description: At 56 seconds, first throw COMPLETED and tightened. Instruments spread wide apart (left upper-left, right upper-right) in classic post-tightening posture. Knot X pattern clearly visible on drain. Drain slit beginning to close.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Excellent frame showing post-tightening spread. The X pattern confirms accurate, centered knot placement. The throw tightening posture shows confident bilateral instrument control.

frame_analyses.description: At 70 seconds, second throw tightening. Both instruments pulling suture tails laterally in mirror posture. Knot visible on drain. Drain slit closing around the knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw completed with good bilateral tightening. The drain edges are visibly approximating.

frame_analyses.description: At 91 seconds, third throw in progress or completing. Right instrument across drain, left instrument at upper left. Knot clearly visible with blue/purple suture forming cross pattern. Drain slit appears mostly closed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw phase. The drain closure is visibly improving through the throw sequence.

frame_analyses.description: At 118 seconds, cutting phase. Both instruments holding suture tails laterally — left pulling left, right pulling right. Suture taut between instruments. Knot visible with dark cross pattern. Drain slit appears CLOSED with good approximation.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting underway. The suture tails are being held taut for scissors. The drain closure looks excellent at this point.

frame_analyses.description: Final frame at 139 seconds. Instruments fully withdrawn. EXCELLENT final result: compact knot clearly visible with purple suture cross pattern. Short, well-trimmed suture tails. Drain slit appears FULLY CLOSED — edges well approximated around the knot. NO VISIBLE GAP. Drain not avulsed, remains centered on suture block. Best visible final result in the monitor-recorded series.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: BEST quality final result assessed to date in the monitor-recorded videos. The combination of closed slit, compact knot, and short tails at 135 seconds represents a significant quality milestone.

knot_assessments.notes: Clear X pattern visible after tightening at 56s. Bilateral spread-tighten posture confirms good technique. Confidence moderate.
Mirror tightening posture at 70s. Drain edges approximating. Confidence moderate.
Third throw completed by ~95s. Drain closure appears complete after this throw. Confidence moderate.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Best drain closure visible in the monitor-recorded series. The final frame shows edges well approximated around a compact knot with no visible gap. Purple suture makes assessment easier than black suture in V1-V10.

technique_summary: V13 confirms the trainee has leveled up. At 135 seconds with NO visible gap and a compact, well-trimmed knot, this represents the best combined speed-and-quality performance in the series. While V12 was 20 seconds faster (115s), V13's superior visible quality (fully closed slit, short tails) may mean a higher true FLS score once penalties are accurately measured. The suture placement at 22 seconds is a new fastest, and all three throw phases are efficient and well-tightened. Cutting at 40 seconds remains the longest phase and the primary target for further improvement. The purple suture significantly improves scoring visibility compared to the black suture used in V1-V10.
improvement_suggestions: Cutting at 40 seconds is still 30% of total time — the single biggest improvement opportunity
V12 (115s) showed the speed ceiling; V13 (135s) shows the quality ceiling — the target is combining both
Consider always using purple/colored suture for AI-scored videos — the visibility improvement is dramatic
All phases except cutting are now near-optimal. Dedicated scissors drills could push total time toward 100 seconds
This appears to be a fresh/early attempt — continue limiting sessions to 3 scored attempts maximum
strengths: BEST quality final result in the series — fully closed slit, compact knot, short tails
NO VISIBLE GAP — first confirmed no-gap result since V9
NEW FASTEST suture placement: 22 seconds (beating V12's 25s)
Needle loading stable at 5 seconds — peak efficiency maintained
All three throws completed efficiently (20s, 22s, 23s — total 65s)
Purple suture improves both execution visibility and AI scoring accuracy
135 seconds confirms new baseline well ahead of original V7/V9 peak (142-143s)

key_observations: 135s / 462 FLS — second-fastest time in the series, BEST visible quality
Confirms new skill plateau: V12 (115s) was peak speed, V13 (135s) is peak quality, both far ahead of old V7/V9 plateau (142-143s)
NO GAP — first confirmed complete closure since V9
Suture placement: 22s (new fastest), Needle loading: 5s (matching V12)
Cutting at 40s improved from V10/V11 (50s) but still the primary bottleneck
Purple suture is a game-changer for AI scoring visibility
New reliable performance range: 115-135s / 462-480 FLS when fresh
